texts.name: Warrior
texts.desc:
Masters of close-quarters combat, Warriors unleash devastating attacks powered by their strength.
They build up Rage as they fight, which can be spent to activate powerful class abilities or fuel self-enhancing buffs.
Brutal and relentless, they thrive in the heart of battle.

texts.name: Rogue
texts.desc:
Agile and deadly, Rogues specialize in swift, precise strikes and deceiving tactics.
They generate Combo Points through attacks and skills, which are consumed to unleash powerful finishing moves.
Favoring speed and precision over brute force, Rogues excel at dismantling enemies before they can react.

texts.name: Mage
texts.desc:
Mages are powerful spellcasters who manipulate volatile forces through careful control of their Spark.
Each spell cast consumes Spark, a resource that fuels their magical abilities. But unlike other casters, Mages thrive on momentum : staying above the Flow threshold triggers enhanced effects and bonus power.

texts.name: Priest
texts.desc:
Priests are devoted spellcasters who channel divine power through sacred Prayers, whispered invocations that call upon celestial forces.
Each Prayer builds over time and can be released to unleash a range of holy effects, from healing and shielding to smiting enemies with radiant force. Mastery lies in choosing the right moment to invoke each blessing.

texts.name: Tidal Totem
texts.desc: All it takes to summon the tidal powers is a handful of fishbones and approximately 6 hours.

texts.name: Guild Merchant
texts.desc: All merchants associated with the 7 Circles Guild protect their anonimity by wearing the exact same outfit and mask in every world. It’s also a way to avoid being associated with a specific humanoid family, which would be bad for business.

texts.name: Guild Merchant
texts.desc: All merchants associated with the 7 Circles Guild protect their anonimity by wearing the exact same outfit and mask in every world. It’s also a way to avoid being associated with a specific humanoid family, which would be bad for business.

texts.name: Job Welldon
texts.desc: Known for leading the Crimson Citadel's restoration, Welldon is now a fervent opponent of Inquisitor Chakram's authority. He managed to protect Navellin's autonomy by intimidating the Rams when they tried to take control of it.

texts.name: Hilda Lambert
texts.desc: Known for leading the Crimson Citadel's restoration, Welldon is now a fervent opponent of Inquisitor Chakram's authority. He managed to protect Navellin's autonomy by intimidating the Rams when they tried to take control of it.

texts.name: Punching Bag
texts.desc: An unmoving and unresponsive enemy whose sole purpose is to be the victim of your wrath.

texts.name: Punching Bag Armor
texts.desc: An unmoving and unresponsive enemy whose sole purpose is to be the victim of your wrath.

texts.name: Punching Bag Magic Resistance
texts.desc: An unmoving and unresponsive enemy whose sole purpose is to be the victim of your wrath.

texts.name: Punching Bag(Invulnerable)
texts.desc: An unmoving and unresponsive enemy whose sole purpose is to be the victim of your wrath.

texts.name: Punching Bag (Shield)
texts.desc: An unmoving and unresponsive enemy whose sole purpose is to be the victim of your wrath.

texts.name: Fleeing Bag
texts.desc: A forever running enemy whose sole purpose is to try to keep a specific distance from you.

texts.name: Ally dummy
texts.desc: Useful to test ally based skills

texts.name: Boar
texts.desc: Boars are misunderstood creatures. Farmers hate them, hunters love them, and they feel the exact opposite.

texts.name: Solitary Boar
texts.desc: Boars are misunderstood creatures. Farmers hate them, hunters love them, and they feel the exact opposite.

texts.name: Reclusive Boar
texts.desc: Boars are misunderstood creatures. Farmers hate them, hunters love them, and they feel the exact opposite.

texts.name: Hermitic Boar
texts.desc: Boars are misunderstood creatures. Farmers hate them, hunters love them, and they feel the exact opposite.
A long-time rival of Al Bearenstein, Niels is reputedly really good at something, but humans have never managed to understand what.
texts.name: Niels Boar
Boar Burnham
texts.desc: Dwelling inside secluded caverns, Boar sometimes ventures in the outside world to attack a few unsuspecting humans before going back to his quiet life.

texts.name: Venerer's Hog
texts.desc: Other venerers repeatedly advised her master that training a hog to hunt wasn't a good idea. 10 years later, no one knows where this man is, but his hog is still wreaking havoc in Skover's woods.

texts.name: Crocoboar
texts.desc: This creature looks like an alligator described by an ancient adventurer who never managed to observe one.

texts.name: Skunk
texts.desc: One cannot emerge victorious from a fight against a skunk. One can only emerge stinky and shameful, whatever the outcome is.

texts.name: Unpopular Skunk
texts.desc: One cannot emerge victorious from a fight against a skunk. One can only emerge stinky and shameful, whatever the outcome is.

texts.desc: One cannot emerge victorious from a fight against a skunk. One can only emerge stinky and shameful, whatever the outcome is.

texts.name: Unpopular Invoked Skunk
texts.desc: One cannot emerge victorious from a fight against a skunk. One can only emerge stinky and shameful, whatever the outcome is.

texts.name: Snow Leopard
texts.desc: There aren’t many preys to catch in the mountains, which means this Leopard is probably hungry.

texts.name: Cave Leopard
texts.desc: There aren’t many preys to catch in the mountains, which means this Leopard is probably hungry.

texts.name: Cliff Leopard
texts.desc: There aren’t many preys to catch in the mountains, which means this Leopard is probably hungry.

texts.name: Rock Leopard
texts.desc: There aren’t many preys to catch in the mountains, which means this Leopard is probably hungry.

texts.name: Volcano Leopard
texts.desc: There aren’t many preys to catch in the mountains, which means this Leopard is probably hungry.

texts.name: Hill Leopard
texts.desc: There aren’t many preys to catch in the mountains, which means this Leopard is probably hungry.

texts.name: Wolf
texts.desc: According to a theory defended by most Crimson scholars, wolves used to be dogs.

texts.name: Old Wolf
texts.desc: Wolves get stronger when they eat well, and fiercer when they don't. Somehow, this specimen managed to do both.

texts.name: Feral Wolf
texts.desc: According to a theory defended by most Crimson scholars, wolves used to be dogs.

texts.name: Rugged Old Wolf
texts.desc: Wolves get stronger when they eat well, and fiercer when they don't. Somehow, this specimen managed to do both.

texts.name: Fangandog
texts.desc: The legend of the dancing wolf can be traced back to the Lost Gods Age. No one has ever actually seen a wolf dance, but we always pick one and suggest it might be doing it while no one is looking.

texts.name: Crimson Hound
texts.desc: Contrary to popular belief, Crimson Hounds are not artificially dyed.

texts.name: Carmine Hound
texts.desc: Contrary to popular belief, Crimson Hounds are not artificially dyed.

texts.name: Bloodred Hound
texts.desc: Contrary to popular belief, Crimson Hounds are not artificially dyed.

texts.name: Dogma
texts.desc: Dogma, hound of the blood moon, is the result of Chakram's tireless attempts to crossbreed the canidae of Niflel and Siagarta. Whether the experiment was a success will be left for its victims to decide.

texts.name: Nepsid Fighter
texts.desc: In their native Merops, the exact working title of these individuals is "Diplomatic Representative in Occupied Territory", which means they were sent to kick your butt.

texts.name: Adept Nepsid Fighter
texts.desc: In their native Merops, the exact working title of these individuals is "Diplomatic Representative in Occupied Territory", which means they were sent to kick your butt.

texts.name: Fighter of the Lost City
texts.desc: In the lost city of Mayda, Nepsid fighters defeated the humans five centuries ago. Today, their descendents seem determined to repeat the feat.

texts.name: Nepsid Runner
texts.desc: Only people who live underwater would think of “runner” as an impressive name for infantry units.

texts.name: Nimble Nepsid Runner
texts.desc: Only people who live underwater would think of “runner” as an impressive name for infantry units.

texts.name: Agile Nepsid Runner
texts.desc: Only people who live underwater would think of “runner” as an impressive name for infantry units.

texts.name: Runner of the Lost City
texts.desc: In the lost city of Mayda, there isn't that much space to run around, which makes the Nepsid runners especially tense.

texts.name: Skuttle Goldengill
texts.desc: Skuttle is fast on the ground. Skuttle is fast underwater. Skuttle is fast when she jumps into the air. The only thing that's not fast about Skuttle is her cruel killing method.

texts.name: Scummy BlackGill
texts.desc: It's no secret that wanted criminals from all around the 9 worlds often end up hiding in Siagarta. Still, Scummy’s reputation as a corrupt individual is so catastrophic he’s even less popular here than among the Meropian court.

texts.name: Nepsid Breakwater
texts.desc: This name is a figure of speech. Water can't be broken. But pretty much everything else can be, and you can count on this guy to make sure it is.

texts.name: Nepsid Wavemonger
texts.desc: Wavemongers are credited with transforming Atlantis from a banal human settlement into an immortal legend.

texts.name: Elder Nepsid Wavemonger
texts.desc: Wavemongers are credited with transforming Atlantis from a banal human settlement into an immortal legend.

texts.name: Educated Nepsid Wavemonger
texts.desc: Wavemongers are credited with transforming Atlantis from a banal human settlement into an immortal legend.

texts.name: Wavemonger of the Lost City
texts.desc: In the lost city of Mayda, annoying human ruins used to get in the way of Nepsid development. They were since taken care of by the wavemongers.

texts.name: Spark-Exposed Wavemonger
texts.desc: Wavemongers are credited with transforming Atlantis from a banal human settlement into an immortal legend.

texts.name: Oracle of the Lost City
texts.desc: In the lost city of Mayda, there is more past than future. This forced the oracles to find a new occupation and start working as historians.

texts.name: Invoked Oracle
texts.desc: One of the Oracles’ tasks is to make the sun rise every morning. Sometimes, it takes them a little longer, but they’ve always succeeded so far.

texts.name: Invoked Wavemonger
texts.desc: Wavemongers are credited with transforming Atlantis from a banal human settlement into an immortal legend.

texts.name: Expert Nepsid Fighter
texts.desc: In their native Merops, the exact working title of these individuals is "Diplomatic Representative in Occupied Territory", which means they were sent to kick your butt.

texts.name: Veteran Nepsid Fighter
texts.desc: In their native Merops, the exact working title of these individuals is "Diplomatic Representative in Occupied Territory", which means they were sent to kick your butt.

texts.name: Acrobatic Nepsid Runner
texts.desc: Only people who live underwater would think of “runner” as an impressive name for infantry units.

texts.name: Supersonic Nepsid Runner
texts.desc: Only people who live underwater would think of “runner” as an impressive name for infantry units.

texts.name: Confident Nepsid Breakwater
texts.desc: This name is a figure of speech. Water can't be broken. But pretty much everything else can be, and you can count on this guy to make sure it is.

texts.name: Proud Nepsid Breakwater
texts.desc: This name is a figure of speech. Water can't be broken. But pretty much everything else can be, and you can count on this guy to make sure it is.

texts.name: Confident Invoked Breakwater
texts.desc: This name is a figure of speech. Water can't be broken. But pretty much everything else can be, and you can count on this guy to make sure it is.

texts.name: Erudite Nepsid Wavemonger
texts.desc: Wavemongers are credited with transforming Atlantis from a banal human settlement into an immortal legend.

texts.name: Omniscient Nepsid Wavemonger
texts.desc: Wavemongers are credited with transforming Atlantis from a banal human settlement into an immortal legend.

texts.name: Nepsid Oracle
texts.desc: One of the Oracles’ tasks is to make the sun rise every morning. Sometimes, it takes them a little longer, but they’ve always succeeded so far.

texts.name: Visionary Nepsid Oracle
texts.desc: One of the Oracles’ tasks is to make the sun rise every morning. Sometimes, it takes them a little longer, but they’ve always succeeded so far.

texts.name: Lord Osis
texts.desc: Lord Osis is the living proof that longevity is not always synonymous with good health. This Nightlord has been feeling old for over a century now, which is probably why the Crimson Rams managed to capture him.

texts.name: Kobold Miner
texts.desc: Kobolds who leave the Underworld of Irukal often expect to finally enjoy sunlight and fresh air. Sadly, Siagarta’s mines are also underground.

texts.name: Freelance Kobold Miner
texts.desc: Kobolds who leave the Underworld of Irukal often expect to finally enjoy sunlight and fresh air. Sadly, Siagarta’s mines are also underground.

texts.name: Kobold Miner of Estrone
texts.desc: In the Kobold mine of Estrone, if you don't have a more specific title, then you're a miner.

texts.name: Gorgon's Hollow Miner
texts.desc: In the mutinied mine of Gorgon's Hollow, aimless miners have nothing left to do besides picking up fights.

texts.name: Spark-Exposed Miner
texts.desc: Kobolds who leave the Underworld of Irukal often expect to finally enjoy sunlight and fresh air. Sadly, Siagarta’s mines are also underground.

texts.name: Kobold Raider
texts.desc: Most Kobolds are qualified to beat up humans intruding their mines, but raiders are the only ones who get paid for this specifically.

texts.name: Kobold Thief
texts.desc: The Kobolds make their own cheese, collect their own Spark and craft their own machinery. For everything else, they rely on this kind of unilateral trade humans like to call thievery.

texts.name: Trustworthy Kobold Thief
texts.desc: The Kobolds make their own cheese, collect their own Spark and craft their own machinery. For everything else, they rely on this kind of unilateral trade humans like to call thievery.

texts.name: Kobold Thief of Estrone
texts.desc: In the Kobold mine of Estrone, thieves are mostly responsible for circulating goods and riches around.

texts.name: Gorgon's Hollow Thief
texts.desc: In the mutinied mine of Gorgon's Hollow, thieves are the only workers who haven't lost their jobs.

texts.name: Kobold Overseer
texts.desc: Kobold workers are autonomous enough not to need a supervisor. However, this is not the case for the many creatures the Kobolds exploit in their farms and mines.

texts.name: Unpredictable Kobold Overseer
texts.desc: Kobold workers are autonomous enough not to need a supervisor. However, this is not the case for the many creatures the Kobolds exploit in their farms and mines.

texts.name: Kobold Overseer of Estrone
texts.desc: In the Kobold mine of Estrone, overseers are making sure intruders stay outside and workers stay inside.

texts.name: Gorgon's Hollow Overseer
texts.desc: In the mutinied mine of Gorgon's Hollow, overseers are mostly overseeing the defeat of their clan at the hands of the golems.

texts.name: Invoked Overseer
texts.desc: Kobold workers are autonomous enough not to need a supervisor. However, this is not the case for the many creatures the Kobolds exploit in their farms and mines.

texts.name: Senior Kobold Miner
texts.desc: Kobolds who leave the Underworld of Irukal often expect to finally enjoy sunlight and fresh air. Sadly, Siagarta’s mines are also underground.

texts.name: Nervous Kobold Raider
texts.desc: Most Kobolds are qualified to beat up humans intruding their mines, but raiders are the only ones who get paid for this specifically.

texts.name: Cleanhanded Kobold Thief
texts.desc: The Kobolds make their own cheese, collect their own Spark and craft their own machinery. For everything else, they rely on this kind of unilateral trade humans like to call thievery.

texts.name: Lunatic Kobold Overseer
texts.desc: Kobold workers are autonomous enough not to need a supervisor. However, this is not the case for the many creatures the Kobolds exploit in their farms and mines.

texts.name: Nightling Terror
texts.desc: Fearing a return of the bloodthirsty humans, Nightling authorities trained these soldiers to scare them away.

texts.name: Nightling Shadow
texts.desc: Preparing excursions to the highworld of Siagarta, Knight Marish trained these soldiers to protect their platoons from light.

texts.name: Crimson Bull
texts.desc: In all humanoid families, about one individual in a hundred is just big. They can crush a skull with their bare hands, but they have a slightly higher risk of cardiovascular disease.

texts.name: Muscular Crimson Bull
texts.desc: In all humanoid families, about one individual in a hundred is just big. They can crush a skull with their bare hands, but they have a slightly higher risk of cardiovascular disease.

texts.name: Crimson Plow Hauler
texts.desc: Hauling a plow through the fields is hard, dull, and bad for your joints. But it's more useful than patrolling with the Crimson army.

texts.name: Unwearied Crimson Plow Hauler
texts.desc: Hauling a plow through the fields is hard, dull, and bad for your joints. But it's more useful than patrolling with the Crimson army.

texts.name: Muscular Crimson Bull
texts.desc: In all humanoid families, about one individual in a hundred is just big. They can crush a skull with their bare hands, but they have a slightly higher risk of cardiovascular disease.

texts.name: Kobold Docker
texts.desc: Is it convenient to be that big when you live in tunnels ? No. Does it make the research of ore any easier ? No. But does it mean your cheese portion is larger ? Also no.

texts.name: Steady Kobold Docker
texts.desc: Is it convenient to be that big when you live in tunnels ? No. Does it make the research of ore any easier ? No. But does it mean your cheese portion is larger ? Also no.

texts.name: Spark-Infused Docker
texts.desc: Is it convenient to be that big when you live in tunnels ? No. Does it make the research of ore any easier ? No. But does it mean your cheese portion is larger ? Also no.

texts.name: Kobold Docker of Estrone
texts.desc: In the Kobold mine of Estrone, everything that's not transported by a chariot is transported by a docker.

texts.name: Gorgon's Hollow Docker
texts.desc: In the mutinied mine of Gorgon's Hollow, dockers are now acting as a lawless and disorganized law enforcement unit.

texts.name: Heraclette
texts.desc: Heraclette is known across Irukal for having completed all 12 labours: every job involved in the creation of a great fondue, from aurock farming to Spark mining.

texts.name: Invoked Docker
texts.desc: Is it convenient to be that big when you live in tunnels ? No. Does it make the research of ore any easier ? No. But does it mean your cheese portion is larger ? Also no.

texts.name: Reliable Kobold Docker
texts.desc: Is it convenient to be that big when you live in tunnels ? No. Does it make the research of ore any easier ? No. But does it mean your cheese portion is larger ? Also no.

texts.name: Unwavering Kobold Docker
texts.desc: Is it convenient to be that big when you live in tunnels ? No. Does it make the research of ore any easier ? No. But does it mean your cheese portion is larger ? Also no.

texts.name: Nepsid Whale
texts.desc: The Nepsids have tried by all means to make gigantism more frequent. However, it only resulted in making their entire population bald.

texts.name: Spark-Exposed Whale
texts.desc: The Nepsids have tried by all means to make gigantism more frequent. However, it only resulted in making their entire population bald.

texts.name: Nepsid Whale of the Lost City
texts.desc: In the lost city of Mayda, there is a shortage of construction jobs, but being twice as tall as the other candidates gives whales a substantial advantage.

texts.name: TODO_Nepsid_Whale_POI2
texts.desc: In the lost city of Mayda, there is a shortage of construction jobs, but being twice as tall as the other candidates gives whales a substantial advantage.

texts.name: Slippery Invoked Whale
texts.desc: The Nepsids have tried by all means to make gigantism more frequent. However, it only resulted in making their entire population bald.

texts.name: Slippery Nepsid Whale
texts.desc: The Nepsids have tried by all means to make gigantism more frequent. However, it only resulted in making their entire population bald.

texts.name: Gilliver
texts.desc: Gilliver is a traveler. In the current state of Nepsid politics, this courtesy title means he's a warrior looking for people to exterminate and riches to plunder.

texts.name: Slick Nepsid Whale
texts.desc: The Nepsids have tried by all means to make gigantism more frequent. However, it only resulted in making their entire population bald.

texts.name: Oiled Nepsid Whale
texts.desc: The Nepsids have tried by all means to make gigantism more frequent. However, it only resulted in making their entire population bald.

texts.name: Crab
texts.desc: "This is the biggest crab I've ever seen", said the adventurer as an even bigger one approached them from behind.

texts.name: Fiddler Crab
texts.desc: "This is the biggest crab I've ever seen", said the adventurer as an even bigger one approached them from behind.

texts.name: Snapping Crab
texts.desc: "This is the biggest crab I've ever seen", said the adventurer as an even bigger one approached them from behind.

texts.name: Virtuoso Crab
texts.desc: "This is the biggest crab I've ever seen", said the adventurer as an even bigger one approached them from behind.

texts.name: Solist Crab
texts.desc: "This is the biggest crab I've ever seen", said the adventurer as an even bigger one approached them from behind.

texts.name: Krabby Jacob
texts.desc: Krabby is rumored to be a mollusc in disguise. But his very convincing crab shell and even more convincing claw strokes have made this rumor impossible to verify.

texts.name: Crab of the Lost City
texts.desc: "This is the biggest crab I've ever seen", said the adventurer as an even bigger one approached them from behind.

texts.name: Snapping Invoked Crab
texts.desc: "This is the biggest crab I've ever seen", said the adventurer as an even bigger one approached them from behind.

texts.name: Virtuoso Invoked Crab
texts.desc: "This is the biggest crab I've ever seen", said the adventurer as an even bigger one approached them from behind.

texts.name: Spark-Infused Crab
texts.desc: "This is the biggest crab I've ever seen", said the adventurer as an even bigger one approached them from behind.

texts.name: Abyss Crab
texts.desc: "This is the biggest crab I've ever seen", said the adventurer as an even bigger one approached them from behind.

texts.name: Belzebubbles
texts.desc: Before meeting Belzebubbles, humans thought of "ridiculous" and "fearsome" as two mutually exclusive traits.

texts.name: Kraba the Sorceress
texts.desc: Kraba is believed by many to turn fishermen into crabs. This claim cannot be verified but it is intimidating, and negatively impacts crab meat consumption in the region.

texts.name: Fiddle Crabstro
texts.desc: Although he's not a real fiddler crab, the "Crab Maestro" is such a galvanizing musician he inspired his kind to revolt against the Nepsids and embrace piracy.

texts.name: Crustacean
texts.desc: If there’s not a pirate or a Nepsid on this creature's back, there’s probably one in its stomach.

texts.name: Spark-Infused Crustacean
texts.desc: If there’s not a pirate or a Nepsid on this creature's back, there’s probably one in its stomach.

texts.name: Voracious Crustacean
texts.desc: If there’s not a pirate or a Nepsid on this creature's back, there’s probably one in its stomach.

texts.name: Carcinos the Proud
texts.desc: Carcinos was once king of the crabs. One would object that crabs don't have a king, but I would answer that it is entirely Carcinos' fault.

texts.name: Apix Harvester
texts.desc: This bee might look abnormally big, but nowadays it's the most normal a bee can get.

texts.name: Bibulous Apix Harvester
texts.desc: This bee might look abnormally big, but nowadays it's the most normal a bee can get.

texts.name: Tipsy Apix Harvester
texts.desc: This bee might look abnormally big, but nowadays it's the most normal a bee can get.

texts.name: Hivetree Harvester
texts.desc: In the Hivetree of Honeywoods, there is no flower to pollinate, which means harvesters are mostly pretending to work.

texts.name: Spark-Infused Harvester
texts.desc: This bee might look abnormally big, but nowadays it's the most normal a bee can get.

texts.name: Spark-Exposed Harvester
texts.desc: This bee might look abnormally big, but nowadays it's the most normal a bee can get.

texts.name: Beelial
texts.desc: It is rare for a Honeybee to live more than a few months, which makes it rather concerning that the oldest Pilgrim records of Beelial date back decades.

texts.name: Bibulous Invoked Harvester
texts.desc: This bee might look abnormally big, but nowadays it's the most normal a bee can get.

texts.name: Intoxicated Apix Harvester
texts.desc: This bee might look abnormally big, but nowadays it's the most normal a bee can get.

texts.name: Blackbird Harpy
texts.desc: The Harpies are not a distinct humanoid family. They are just so much stronger, smarter and more organized than their male counterparts that they ended up forming an elite group within the Swarowls.

texts.name: Apix Watcher
texts.desc: In Apix vocabulary, "watching" usually also implies "punishin", "slaying" and "making sure humans never come back".

texts.name: Hivetree Watcher
texts.desc: In the Hivetree of Honeywoods, if a watcher sees you, it means you're not where you should be.

texts.name: Spark-Exposed Builder
texts.desc: When the bees discovered that piercing combs and piercing enemy skin were very similar activities, they merged their construction and defense departments.

texts.name: Honey Mucha
texts.desc: On her spare time, Mucha is an outstanding painter. But her main occupation is to slay fearsome enemies with her trusted blade, and absorb their souls.

texts.name: Apix Builder
texts.desc: When the bees discovered that piercing combs and piercing enemy skin were very similar activities, they merged their construction and defense departments.

texts.name: Hivetree Builder
texts.desc: In the Hivetree of Honeywoods, builders have to keep up with the abnormally quick growth of their hive's walls.

texts.name: Apix Defender
texts.desc: Contrary to popular belief, Apix defenders mostly attack.

texts.name: Apix Royal Guard
texts.desc: The morphological disparity between bees is not natural: the higher they rank, the more Spark they're allowed to consume, and the more humanoid they get.

texts.name: Royal Guard of the Hivetree
texts.desc: In the Hivetree of Honeywoods, some royal guards are actual royals, somehow guarding themselves.

texts.name: Honey Zucca
texts.desc: Zucca initially enlisted in the army after noticing how popular the guardians were among worker bees. She now seems to be taking the job seriously, in her own way.

texts.name: Invoked Royal Guard
texts.desc: The morphological disparity between bees is not natural: the higher they rank, the more Spark they're allowed to consume, and the more humanoid they get.

texts.name: Spark-Infused Builder
texts.desc: When the bees discovered that piercing combs and piercing enemy skin were very similar activities, they merged their construction and defense departments.

texts.name: Overzealous Apix Watcher
texts.desc: In Apix vocabulary, "watching" usually also implies "punishin", "slaying" and "making sure humans never come back".

texts.name: Overworked Apix Builder
texts.desc: When the bees discovered that piercing combs and piercing enemy skin were very similar activities, they merged their construction and defense departments.

texts.name: Overprotective Apix Defender
texts.desc: Contrary to popular belief, Apix defenders mostly attack.

texts.name: Overqualified Apix Royal Guard
texts.desc: The morphological disparity between bees is not natural: the higher they rank, the more Spark they're allowed to consume, and the more humanoid they get.

texts.name: Bartlebee
texts.desc: In a clan known for its extreme work ethic, Bartlebee stands out as a notorious slacker. Thankfully, bodyguards aren't supposed to do much anyway.

texts.name: Spark-Infused Guard
texts.desc: The morphological disparity between bees is not natural: the higher they rank, the more Spark they're allowed to consume, and the more humanoid they get.

texts.name: Spark-Infused Guard
texts.desc: The morphological disparity between bees is not natural: the higher they rank, the more Spark they're allowed to consume, and the more humanoid they get.

texts.name: Notorious Bee
texts.desc: Messenger of a generation, Bee is always followed by a cohort of followers known as the Junior M.A.F.I.A (Melliferous Apprentices Following the Idol of Apix).

texts.name: Invoked Defender
texts.desc: Contrary to popular belief, Apix defenders mostly attack.

texts.name: Overworked Invoked Builder
texts.desc: When the bees discovered that piercing combs and piercing enemy skin were very similar activities, they merged their construction and defense departments.

texts.name: Rice Seedling
texts.desc: When Spark ledges overflow, they permeate the soil and bring crops to life, which usually attracts weak adventurers looking for easy targets.

texts.name: Black Rice Seedling
texts.desc: These are actually the regular Rice Seedlings. The light ones have albinism, which only became widespread after the Crimson Rams tried using vinegar as fertilizer.

texts.name: Onion Seedling
texts.desc: Not many people enjoy the taste of these Onion Seedlings. But they're less dangerous cooked than alive, so we eat them anyway.

texts.name: Controversial  Onion Seedling
texts.desc: Not many people enjoy the taste of these Onion Seedlings. But they're less dangerous cooked than alive, so we eat them anyway.

texts.name: Garlic Seedling
texts.desc: Although garlic is known for its many virtues, I would not recommend approaching this creature to anyone who values their health.

texts.name: Fragrant Garlic Seedling
texts.desc: Although garlic is known for its many virtues, I would not recommend approaching this creature to anyone who values their health.

texts.name: Turnip Seedling
texts.desc: A farmer who once tried to prepare a broth with live Turnip Seedlings is credited with inventing the first and deadliest bubble bath in history.

texts.name: Lucrative Turnip Seedling
texts.desc: A farmer who once tried to prepare a broth with live Turnip Seedlings is credited with inventing the first and deadliest bubble bath in history.

texts.name: Invoked Rice Seedling
texts.desc: When Spark ledges overflow, they permeate the soil and bring crops to life, which usually attracts weak adventurers looking for easy targets.

texts.name: Invoked Onion Seedling
texts.desc: Not many people enjoy the taste of these Onion Seedlings. But they're less dangerous cooked than alive, so we eat them anyway.

texts.name: Beet the Maul
texts.desc: Beetroots are known to be bloody, but Maul earned its nickname through repeated, spectacular bursts of rage and violence.

texts.name: Seed Barrett
texts.desc: Barrett has been called a bad seed by Crimson farmhands, but many seedlings are still rooting for it.

texts.name: Seed Vicious
texts.desc: Everything vegetables do is out of norm, because they're not supposed to do anything. Except growing, which is the only thing Vicious has stopped doing.

texts.name: Turnip Arker
texts.desc: In ball games, turnips traditionally played as the ball. That was until Turnip Arker reversed the balance of power.

texts.name: Garlic Hooper
texts.desc: Garlic Hooper has become a role model for many seedlings, embodying "the strong, silent type". Most garlic plants are silent, but not all of them can smell that strong.

texts.name: Charles Cabbage
texts.desc: Charles is a polymath who knows its onions in all domains of seedling science: sour grapes, hot potatoes and grassroots organizing.

texts.name: Jared Lettuce
texts.desc: Jared's efforts to cultivate a down-to-earth reputation never bore fruit. Ever since his salad days, people have deemed it full of beans.

texts.name: Light Elemental
texts.desc: Flying enemy

texts.name: Sparkle
texts.desc: This used to be a golem. But millenia of elemental pressure have hardened its crystal heart and destroyed pretty much everything else.

texts.name: Loamy Sparkle
texts.desc: This used to be a golem. But millenia of elemental pressure have hardened its crystal heart and destroyed pretty much everything else.

texts.name: Sparkle of Estrone
texts.desc: In the Kobold mine of Estrone, golems are exploited, but their sparkle counterparts remained free thanks to their absolute inability to do any work.

texts.name: Invoked Sparkle
texts.desc: This used to be a golem. But millenia of elemental pressure have hardened its crystal heart and destroyed pretty much everything else.

texts.name: Loamy Invoked Sparkle
texts.desc: This used to be a golem. But millenia of elemental pressure have hardened its crystal heart and destroyed pretty much everything else.

texts.name: Spark-Exposed Sparkle
texts.desc: This used to be a golem. But millenia of elemental pressure have hardened its crystal heart and destroyed pretty much everything else.

texts.name: Spark-Filled Sparkle
texts.desc: This used to be a golem. But millenia of elemental pressure have hardened its crystal heart and destroyed pretty much everything else.

texts.name: Gorgon's Hollow Sparkle
texts.desc: In the mutinied mine of Gorgon's Hollow, Sparkles are the mute, inert and indifferent witnesses of spectacular chaos.

texts.name: Naya Sparkle
texts.desc: These creatures have often been confused with urchins, but there are two big differences: these are hundreds of years old, and they will actively try to kill you.

texts.name: New Atlaan Sparkle
texts.desc: In the underwater city of New Atlaan, sparkles are used as decorative deadly weapons.

texts.name: Invoked Naya Sparkle
texts.desc: These creatures have often been confused with urchins, but there are two big differences: these are hundreds of years old, and they will actively try to kill you.

texts.name: Naya Sparkle
texts.desc: These creatures have often been confused with urchins, but there are two big differences: these are hundreds of years old, and they will actively try to kill you.

texts.name: Spiky Naya Sparkle
texts.desc: These creatures have often been confused with urchins, but there are two big differences: these are hundreds of years old, and they will actively try to kill you.
This rare gemstone has the property of moisturizing skin, which hardly compensates for its tendency to drown people.
texts.name: Aquamarine Sparkle
Aoyl Sparkle
texts.desc: When people talk about the eye of a storm, they usually mean something like this.

texts.name: Lightweight Aoyl Sparkle
texts.desc: When people talk about the eye of a storm, they usually mean something like this.

texts.name: Invoked Aoyl Sparkle
texts.desc: When people talk about the eye of a storm, they usually mean something like this.

texts.name: Pyrh Sparkle
texts.desc: When youngsters in Siagarta say "this is fire", what they really mean is "run".

texts.name: Molten Pyrh Sparkle
texts.desc: When youngsters in Siagarta say "this is fire", what they really mean is "run".

texts.name: Pyrh Sparkle
texts.desc: When youngsters in Siagarta say "this is fire", what they really mean is "run".

texts.name: Molten Invoked Pyrh Sparkle
texts.desc: When youngsters in Siagarta say "this is fire", what they really mean is "run".

texts.name: Golem
texts.desc: If you break a golem's husk, a new one will start to aggregate around its little Spark soul. It might take a few thousand years, though.

texts.name: Solid  Golem
texts.desc: If you break a golem's husk, a new one will start to aggregate around its little Spark soul. It might take a few thousand years, though.

texts.name: Smeagolem
texts.desc: Ever since this poor creature found a Kobold's earring in the depths of Enripit, it convinced itself that someone would come and steal that treasure.

texts.name: Goledeneye
texts.desc: Originally trained by the Kobolds as a deadly, precise and highly controllable weapon, Goledeneye is neither controllable nor precise, but the Kobolds soon learned it was indeed deadly.

texts.name: Blankenstein
texts.desc: Among the experiments by which the Kobolds tried to create superintelligent golems, Blankenstein is not one of the least unsuccessful ones.

texts.name: Gorgon's Hollow Golem
texts.desc: In the mutinied mine of Gorgon's Hollow, the golems have organized a strike, and it won't be a surgical one.

texts.name: Invoked Magma Golem
texts.desc: As the golem proverb goes, "The floor is lava. You is floor. Lava is you."

texts.name: Invoked Golem
texts.desc: If you break a golem's husk, a new one will start to aggregate around its little Spark soul. It might take a few thousand years, though.

texts.name: Solid Invoked Golem
texts.desc: If you break a golem's husk, a new one will start to aggregate around its little Spark soul. It might take a few thousand years, though.

texts.name: Golcano
texts.desc: Golcano has been alive for many centuries. Yet, no one ever taught it yet that drooling magma is bad manners.

texts.name: Pyrh Golem
texts.desc: If you break a golem's husk, a new one will start to aggregate around its little Spark soul. It might take a few thousand years, though.

texts.name: Incandescent Pyrh Golem
texts.desc: If you break a golem's husk, a new one will start to aggregate around its little Spark soul. It might take a few thousand years, though.

texts.name: Blaze Rascal
texts.desc: According to legend, Rascal's head was set ablaze by excess thinking. As another flaming golem once said: "Cogito ergo soot"

texts.name: Grill Arson
texts.desc: Known for its fiery words and ardent performances, Arson has become a hot topic in the world of volcanoes.

texts.name: Invoked Pyrh Golem
texts.desc: As the golem proverb goes, "The floor is lava. You is floor. Lava is you."

texts.name: Incandescent Invoked Pyrh Golem
texts.desc: As the golem proverb goes, "The floor is lava. You is floor. Lava is you."

texts.name: Aoyl Golem
texts.desc: If you break a golem's husk, a new one will start to aggregate around its little Spark soul. It might take a few thousand years, though.

texts.name: Whispering Aoyl Golem
texts.desc: If you break a golem's husk, a new one will start to aggregate around its little Spark soul. It might take a few thousand years, though.

texts.name: Breezy Aoyl Golem
texts.desc: If you break a golem's husk, a new one will start to aggregate around its little Spark soul. It might take a few thousand years, though.

texts.name: Invoked Aoyl Golem
texts.desc: If you break a golem's husk, a new one will start to aggregate around its little Spark soul. It might take a few thousand years, though.

texts.name: Golatea
texts.desc: Although Golatea is made of stone and incapable of speech, its liveliness once captured the heart of famous golem sculptor Magmalion.

texts.name: Jimmy Crater
texts.desc: Like most craters, Jimmy is mostly recognized by its emptiness.

texts.name: Spark-Infused Golem
texts.desc: If you break a golem's husk, a new one will start to aggregate around its little Spark soul. It might take a few thousand years, though.

texts.name: Slime King
texts.desc: The slimes have no borders but they have many kings, which has often caused political turmoil in their recent history.

texts.name: Night Slime
texts.desc: “Keep your eye in the ball”, a wise Night Slime once said.

texts.name: Knight Slime
texts.desc: While Siagarta’s slimes were mostly left to themselves for the last centuries, Niflel’s slimes have spent most of their time around Nightlings, and it shows.

texts.name: Green Slime
texts.desc: While most vertebrates use their skulls to protect their viscous organs, slimes have taken the opposite path.

texts.name: Sap Slime
texts.desc: Contrary to popular belief, these slimes are not apricot-flavored.

texts.name: Ripe Sap Slime
texts.desc: Contrary to popular belief, these slimes are not apricot-flavored.

texts.name: Barrel-aged Sap Slime
texts.desc: Contrary to popular belief, these slimes are not apricot-flavored.

texts.name: Spark-Exposed Slime
texts.desc: While most vertebrates use their skulls to protect their viscous organs, slimes have taken the opposite path.

texts.name: Invoked Slime
texts.desc: The exact composition of slimes is always slightly doubtful, but these ones are a complete mystery.

texts.name: Barrel-aged Invoked Slime
texts.desc: The exact composition of slimes is always slightly doubtful, but these ones are a complete mystery.

texts.name: Ripe Invoked Slime
texts.desc: The exact composition of slimes is always slightly doubtful, but these ones are a complete mystery.

texts.name: Blob Dylan
texts.desc: Blob's name has become a synonym for freedom and change across the Slimes' Kingdom. They still don't have freedom, and not much has really changed, but at least they have Blob Dylan.

texts.name: Jelly Lewis
texts.desc: It is not unusual for slimes to provoke laughter, but this one is the first to do it on purpose. Upon discovering that its talent was best appreciated by humans, Lewis started using it as a distraction to devour imprudent travelers.

texts.name: Royal Jelly Slime
texts.desc: Disguising itself as an Apix larva, this slime managed to get fed by worker bees for several months.

texts.name: Honey Slime
texts.desc: It is possible to drink the slime with a straw and leave its skull defenseless. That's not to say I recommend it.

texts.name: Honey Slime of the Hivetree
texts.desc: It is possible to drink the slime with a straw and leave its skull defenseless. That's not to say I recommend it.

texts.name: Honey Slime of the Hivetree
texts.desc: It is possible to drink the slime with a straw and leave its skull defenseless. That's not to say I recommend it.

texts.name: Apostate Spirit of Stone
texts.desc: When the Apostates left the Herald order, the most prideful of them embraced the coat of Stone. They erected large temples to welcome the Nightlings and give them the acceptance the Heralds refused them.

texts.name: Apostate Spirit of Dust
texts.desc: When the Apostates left the Herald order, the most sorrowful of them embraced the coat of Dust. They loomed in the ruins, trying to prevent the return of humankind and its exploitation at the hands of the Heralds.

texts.name: Invoked Spirit of Dust
texts.desc: When the Apostates left the Herald order, the most sorrowful of them embraced the coat of Dust. They loomed in the ruins, trying to prevent the return of humankind and its exploitation at the hands of the Heralds.

texts.name: Apostate Spirit of Night
texts.desc: When the Apostates left the Herald order, the most vengeful of them embraced the coat of Night. They started cultivating shadows in hope of countering the Heralds' intolerable light.

texts.name: Herald Spirit
texts.desc: Carrying out the orders of the Avatars, the Herald Spirits mostly act as trainers for their human Disciples.

texts.name: Crimson Soldier
texts.desc: Not all Crimson Soldiers are violent fanatics, but they all have to act as such and often end up convincing themselves.

texts.name: Pious Crimson Soldier
texts.desc: Not all Crimson Soldiers are violent fanatics, but they all have to act as such and often end up convincing themselves.

texts.name: Crimson Captain Agamemnon
texts.desc: Agamemnon would just be a normal captain if it wasn't for his passionate interest in Primeval Age pottery.

texts.name: Ram Page
texts.desc: Although he's no longer a page, Ram will forever bear the moniker he earned himself when he accidentally wreaked havoc in a Nepsid camp and miraculously survived.

texts.name: Crimson Archer
texts.desc: At first, the Rams thought it was dishonoring to use long-range weapons. It didn't take them long to decide that defeat was even more dishonoring.

texts.name: Crimson Captain
texts.desc: The principal quality expected of a Crimson Captain is their ability to enforce absurd decisions with enough confidence to prevent their subordinates from noticing what's wrong.

texts.name: Crimson Farmhand
texts.desc: Visitors in Skover often wonder why the region's farmers are all part of the Crimson army. After meeting the region's turnips, they usually stop asking questions.
When he left the outlaw life to settle down as a celeryman, Pumpkintaro had to change his name. The new one isn't perfect, but it is what it is.
texts.name: Celeryman Pumpkintaro
Bucolic Crimson Farmhand
texts.desc: Visitors in Skover often wonder why the region's farmers are all part of the Crimson army. After meeting the region's turnips, they usually stop asking questions.

texts.name: Devout Crimson Soldier
texts.desc: Not all Crimson Soldiers are violent fanatics, but they all have to act as such and often end up convincing themselves.

texts.name: Fervent Crimson Soldier
texts.desc: Not all Crimson Soldiers are violent fanatics, but they all have to act as such and often end up convincing themselves.

texts.name: Obsessive Crimson Soldier
texts.desc: Not all Crimson Soldiers are violent fanatics, but they all have to act as such and often end up convincing themselves.

texts.name: Observant Crimson Archer
texts.desc: At first, the Rams thought it was dishonoring to use long-range weapons. It didn't take them long to decide that defeat was even more dishonoring.

texts.name: Sighted Crimson Archer
texts.desc: At first, the Rams thought it was dishonoring to use long-range weapons. It didn't take them long to decide that defeat was even more dishonoring.

texts.name: Eagle-eyed Crimson Archer
texts.desc: At first, the Rams thought it was dishonoring to use long-range weapons. It didn't take them long to decide that defeat was even more dishonoring.

texts.name: William Tail
texts.desc: When High Inquisitor Chakram ordered Tail to bow before the almighty Amon Ram, he misinterpreted these words and became an archer.

texts.name: Charismatic Crimson Captain
texts.desc: The principal quality expected of a Crimson Commander is their ability to enforce absurd decisions with enough confidence to prevent their subordinates from noticing what's wrong.

texts.name: Charming Crimson Captain
texts.desc: The principal quality expected of a Crimson Commander is their ability to enforce absurd decisions with enough confidence to prevent their subordinates from noticing what's wrong.

texts.name: Magnetic Crimson Captain
texts.desc: The principal quality expected of a Crimson Commander is their ability to enforce absurd decisions with enough confidence to prevent their subordinates from noticing what's wrong.

texts.name: Captain Ramshackle
texts.desc: Though she got her nickname from the ramshackle ruins the Rams used to live in, this captain kept it because of the terrible yoke she imposes on her subordinates.

texts.name: Captain Wolfram
texts.desc: The Crimson Rams used to have 2 problems: wolf attacks and low meat reserves. Now that Wolfram taught them to eat wolves, they have other, more complicated problems.

texts.name: Crimson Captain Miranda
texts.desc: Miranda would just be a normal captain if it wasn't for her exceptional dancing skills.

texts.name: Crimson Captain Sensabille
texts.desc: Sensabille would just be a normal captain if it wasn't for her persistent hearing problems.

texts.name: Crimson Captain Clover
texts.desc: Clover would just be a normal captain if it wasn't for her alleged spying activities.

texts.name: Crimson Captain Carmine
texts.desc: Carmine would just be a normal captain if it wasn't for his thwarted artistic ambitions.

texts.name: Crimson Captain Hector
texts.desc: Hector would just be a normal captain if it wasn't for the unfortunate marriage that made him the butt of countless jokes.

texts.name: Crimson Captain Ben
texts.desc: Ben would just be a normal captain if it wasn't for his habit to play with the figurines of the strategy table.

texts.name: Crimson Geomancer
texts.desc: It's surprising that the Crimson Rams, who lived on a tiny island for most of their history, specialized in the earth magic of Kyre. But don't bring up the subject around them.

texts.name: Hardheaded Crimson Geomancer
texts.desc: It's surprising that the Crimson Rams, who lived on a tiny island for most of their history, specialized in the earth magic of Kyre. But don't bring up the subject around them.

texts.name: Shrewd Crimson Geomancer
texts.desc: It's surprising that the Crimson Rams, who lived on a tiny island for most of their history, specialized in the earth magic of Kyre. But don't bring up the subject around them.

texts.name: Crimson Beekeeper
texts.desc: Now that the bees have spent 5 centuries crafting honey with no human interference, beekeeping has become a much tougher job than it once was.

texts.name: Beekept Crimson
texts.desc: This used to be a beekeeper but the tables have turned, now.

texts.name: Nepsilon
texts.desc: Nepsilon, Envoy of Emperor Nephisto in the land of Skover, doesn’t dislike humans personally. She’s just tasked with ensuring all areas previously inhabited by them are now inhabited by Nepsids.

sequences.comment: Moves to Point A  and uses the Water Breath skill

texts.name: Reblochonk
texts.desc: Since the Kobolds' sports are very similar to their work, Reblochonk is both the best employee under Ratsar's authority and the regional cheese rolling champion.

texts.name: Gatsbee
texts.desc: A rare successful outsider within the Apix clan, Gatsbee proved their worth by smuggling royal jelly across the continent.

texts.name: Crabgantua

lines.text: $icon(Warning) Take refuge on the Shell Fragments! $icon(Warning)

texts.desc: Crabgantua, Creature of the Nepsids, was bred and trained to become a humongous warmount. The first part of the plan was such a success that its tamers got devoured before they could get to the second.

texts.name: Crab of the Lost City
texts.desc: "This is the biggest crab I've ever seen", said the adventurer as an even bigger one approached them from behind.

texts.name: Munster Chuck
texts.desc: Chuck, Operator of the Kobolds’ transportation system, is tasked with making sure no cheese cargo gets lost and all human trespassers do get lost.

texts.name: Lady Bee

lines.text: Stalactites have fallen from the ceiling! Hop on them to get to a cannon.

texts.desc: Lady Bee, most humanoid specimen of the Apix clan, aspires to become the most humanoid of all creatures, once the actual humans have been taken care of.

texts.name: Capriscorn Chakram

lines.text: Chakram is intensifying the Chaos Rifts' activity

namePerPhase.name: Chakram, High Inquisitor of the Crimson Rams

texts.desc: Chakram, Supreme authority of the Crimson Rams, is feared by his followers and trusted by his rivals rather than the opposite.

texts.name: ul'Serous, Herald Avatar

lines.text: Chakram is intensifying the Chaos Rifts' activity

namePerPhase.name: ul'Serous, First Step of the Judgement
ul'Serous, Second Step of the Judgement

texts.desc: ul'Serous, Herald authority of Skover, set out to restore the world's balance by helping the Sunlings make up their lost ground.

texts.name: Queen Honeyzabeth
texts.desc: Honeyzabeth, Insect queen of Skover and mother of all bees in the Apix clan, is said to have claimed as many lives as she has given.

texts.name: King Ratsar
texts.desc: Ratsar, First of the Kobold kings to settle in Siagarta, is notoriously driven by his imperishable spite. A sentiment rooted in lactose intolerance as well as human intolerance.

sequences.comment: Requests the carts with associated mobs and move next to them, on arrival, will start casting the absorption skill. Ended upon absorption skill end.

texts.name: Sparkle
texts.desc: This used to be a golem. But millenia of elemental pressure have hardened its crystal heart and destroyed pretty much everything else.

texts.name: Buttontail
texts.desc: The flowery tail of this hare seems to blossom when they reach adulthood. Its function remains somewhat unclear.

texts.name: Grassflopper
texts.desc: Masters of camouflage, these frogs are largely ignored by carnivorous predators, but have made themselves a target for unsuspecting ruminants.
